channel.title: Retirement on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Retirement on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: Retirement: Support for Python-2.7; 3.8 and PowerShell- 7.1; 7.2 will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description:
Azure Automation will stop supporting Python 2.7, Python 3.8, and PowerShell 7.1/7.2 starting October 1, 2026. Runbooks may still run, but these runtimes will no longer receive security updates or bug fixes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot Billing Preview app will be retired on August 3
item.description:
GitHub will retire the Copilot Billing Preview app on August 3, 2026. Users who rely on it for Copilot spend review are directed to use built-in GitHub visibility instead.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Models is being fully retired on July 30, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Models will be fully retired on July 30, 2026. GitHub had already announced in June that it was closing the service to new customers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Migrate from Azure Blueprints by January 31, 2027
item.description:
Azure Blueprints retirement has been extended from July 11, 2026 to January 31, 2027. Phased retirement starts July 31, 2026, when creating new blueprint definitions will no longer be supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Models is no longer available to new customers
item.description:
GitHub is retiring GitHub Models. As an initial step, new customers can no longer use it, while existing organizations or enterprises that have already used it are not affected by this announcement.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Av2-series, F-series, Fs-series, Fsv2-series, G-series, Gs-series, and Lsv2-series Virtual Machines for Azure Batch pools
item.description:
Azure Compute will retire several VM series used by Azure Batch pools on November 15, 2028. After that date, new Batch pools cannot use these series and existing pools must be moved to supported VM types.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: D-series, Ds-series, Dv2-series, Dsv2-series, and Ls-series Virtual Machines for Azure Batch pools
item.description:
Azure Compute will retire D-series, Ds-series, Dv2-series, Dsv2-series, and Ls-series VMs for Azure Batch pools on May 1, 2028. After that date, new Batch pools cannot be created with these VM series.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: GPv1 and Legacy Blob storage account creation
item.description:
Azure is retiring all General purpose v1 (GPv1) storage accounts, including legacy blob storage accounts. The notice says this is part of ongoing Azure Storage portfolio changes aimed at improving performance, scalability, and cost efficiency.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Load Balancer Inbound NAT rule version 1 for Azure VMSS (aka Inbound NAT Pools)
item.description:
Microsoft narrowed the scope of an Azure Load Balancer retirement notice. The retirement now applies only to Inbound NAT rules version 1 resources for single VMs, not VMSS NAT pools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Synapse Link for Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL
item.description:
Azure Synapse Link for Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL is being retired. New Azure Cosmos DB NoSQL accounts cannot enable it after March 31, 2026, and existing customers are supported until its end of life on March 31, 2029.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure VPN Client for Linux (Preview) will be retired on August 31, 2026
item.description:
Azure VPN Client for Linux (Preview) will be retired on August 31, 2026 because it has remained in preview and has no path to general availability. The notice says the retirement is part of aligning Azure networking services with current security and reliability standards.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Reserved Virtual Machines Instances for select VM series
item.description:
Azure will retire certain Reserved VM Instance options for select VM series starting July 1, 2026. One-year purchases and renewals will stop for listed series, and the notice also indicates broader retirement of one-year and three-year reserved instance offerings for those series.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Prompt Flow in Azure ML and Foundry
item.description:
Prompt Flow in Microsoft Foundry and Azure Machine Learning will be retired on April 20, 2027 and replaced by Microsoft Agent Framework. The notice says the replacement is a single orchestration platform with new capabilities, but the provided excerpt does not include migration details.

  • Source: Azure Updates
